## 📊 IDENTITY & PROTOCOL DIAGNOSIS

### The Core Transmission Problem

A remote site needs to answer three questions from a single round-trip, in the order of milliseconds, with no out-of-band context:

1. **Who is this agent?** — A cryptographic identity anchored to a public key, not a User-Agent string.
2. **Who authorized it, and for what?** — A human-signed grant whose scope is verifiable, not a self-asserted claim.
3. **Is that grant still valid and unspoiled?** — Live revocation, nonce freshness, chain integrity.

The transmission channel is **RFC 9421 HTTP Message Signatures** (`web-bot-auth`) for the request signing layer, combined with a **W3C Verifiable Credential envelope** carrying the Mandate and its attenuation chain. The site verifies offline (no callback to AgentGrid required) using keys fetched from a `.well-known` directory; live revocation checks are an **optimization**, not a dependency, so a site can still trust a signed agent during an AgentGrid outage.

### Cryptographic Primitives in Use

| Layer | Primitive | Purpose |
|---|---|---|
| Agent identity | Ed25519 over Curve25519 | Self-certifying `did:key` method — identifier *is* the public key |
| Mandate signing | Ed25519 + RFC 8785 (JCS) | Canonical serialization, deterministic across platforms |
| Request signing | Ed25519 + RFC 9421 | Signs the HTTP message components into the wire |
| Credential envelope | W3C VC 2.0 with `DataIntegrityProof` | Standard, parseable by any VC stack; embeds the Mandate |
| Attenuation proofs | Hash-chained, monotonically-narrowing scope fields | Subset relation is provable; not asserted |
| Revocation | W3C `StatusList2021` (bitstring) embedded in the credential | O(1) verification; no per-credential callback |
| Transport security | TLS 1.3 | Non-negotiable baseline |

### The Attenuation Chain — Why It Carries the Safety Story

A site does not only learn *"this agent claims to be allowed to do X"* — it learns **the bounded human envelope from which X was carved**. The chain root is a passport with `operatorDid = did:key:HUMAN`; that operator's keypair signs the root mandate; root mandates spawn child mandates whose scope is mathematically verified to be `⊆` the parent's. When the site receives a child-issued request, it walks the chain and confirms:

- Every signature is valid (Ed25519 verify against the linked issuer's public key).
- Every scope is a strict subset of its parent (no widening at any hop).
- The depth never exceeds the parent's `maxDelegationDepth`.
- Time bounds are monotonically tightening (`child.notBefore ≥ parent.notBefore`, `child.expires ≤ parent.expires`).
- The chain terminates at an operator whose public key is pinned in the agent's `.well-known` directory.

This is the mathematical proof that a compromised sub-agent cannot widen its own authority — the chain will not verify. It is also what distinguishes a *governed* agent from a *provisioned* one. Provisioning tools hand a child a credential; AgentGrid hands a child a credential whose **authority boundary is a property of the chain**, cryptographically enforced, with the operator as the unforgeable root.

## 🛠️ DETAILED TECHNICAL SPECIFICATION

### 1. The Agent Passport (re-stated for completeness)

```jsonc
{
  "@context": ["https://www.w3.org/ns/did/v1", "https://agentgrid.dev/ns/passport/v1"],
  "id": "did:key:z6MkhaXgBZDvotDkL5257faiztiGiC2QtKLGpbnnEGta2doK",
  "type": "AgentPassport",
  "operator": "did:key:z6MkiY7Ng5cvV4gY5v3jL3k4m2n1p9q8r7s6t5u4v3w2x1y0z",
  "displayName": "AgentGrid Agent — Research",
  "created": "2026-06-12T10:00:00Z",
  "verificationMethod": [{
    "id": "did:key:z6MkhaXgBZDvotDkL5257faiztiGiC2QtKLGpbnnEGta2doK#key-1",
    "type": "Ed25519VerificationKey2020",
    "publicKeyJwk": { "kty": "OKP", "crv": "Ed25519", "x": "..." }
  }],
  "service": [{
    "id": "did:key:z6Mk...#bot-auth",
    "type": "HttpMessageSignaturesDirectory",
    "serviceEndpoint": "https://agents.agentgrid.dev/.well-known/http-message-signatures-directory/did:key:z6MkhaXgBZDvotDkL5257faiztiGiC2QtKLGpbnnEGta2doK"
  }, {
    "id": "did:key:z6Mk...#status",
    "type": "StatusList2021",
    "serviceEndpoint": "https://agents.agentgrid.dev/credentials/status/did:key:z6Mk..."
  }],
  "status": "active",
  "proof": { /* operator's Ed25519 signature over JCS-canonicalized passport body */ }
}
```

### 2. The Mandate Verifiable Credential

```jsonc
{
  "@context": [
    "https://www.w3.org/ns/credentials/v2",
    "https://agentgrid.dev/ns/mandate/v1"
  ],
  "id": "https://agents.agentgrid.dev/mandates/mnd_01HZX9R3K4P",
  "type": ["VerifiableCredential", "AgentGridMandate"],
  "issuer": "did:key:z6MkiY7Ng5cvV4gY5v3jL3k4m2n1p9q8r7s6t5u4v3w2x1y0z",
  "validFrom": "2026-06-12T00:00:00Z",
  "validUntil": "2026-12-31T23:59:59Z",
  "credentialSubject": {
    "id": "did:key:z6MkhaXgBZDvotDkL5257faiztiGiC2QtKLGpbnnEGta2doK",
    "capability": "browse",
    "scope": {
      "allowedDomains": ["*.github.com", "*.wikipedia.org"],
      "deniedDomains": ["bank.example.com"],
      "maxSessionMinutes": 60,
      "trustDomain": "agent-owned"
    },
    "stepUpThreshold": null,
    "delegable": true,
    "maxDelegationDepth": 1,
    "parent": null,
    "chainRoot": "https://agents.agentgrid.dev/mandates/mnd_01HZX8ROOT"
  },
  "credentialStatus": {
    "id": "https://agents.agentgrid.dev/credentials/status/did:key:z6Mk...#0",
    "type": "StatusList2021Entry",
    "statusPurpose": "revocation",
    "statusListIndex": "42",
    "statusListCredential": "https://agents.agentgrid.dev/credentials/status/did:key:z6Mk..."
  },
  "proof": {
    "type": "DataIntegrityProof",
    "cryptosuite": "eddsa-jcs-2022",
    "verificationMethod": "did:key:z6MkiY7N...#key-1",
    "created": "2026-06-12T00:00:00Z",
    "proofPurpose": "assertionMethod",
    "proofValue": "z3Jp7G4n..."
  }
}
```

A child mandate differs by setting `"parent": "https://agents.agentgrid.dev/mandates/mnd_01HZX9R3K4P"`, narrowing every scope field, tightening time bounds, and being signed by the agent's key (not the operator's). The chain then **explicitly carries its parent link** for verifier traversal.

### 3. The HTTP Request — Exact Header Shape (RFC 9421)

When the agent navigates to `https://target-site.com/orders` and clicks a checkout button, the executor emits:

```http
GET /orders/12345 HTTP/1.1
Host: target-site.com
User-Agent: AgentGrid/1.0 (+https://agentgrid.dev/bot)
Accept: application/json
Sec-Agent-Identity: did:key:z6MkhaXgBZDvotDkL5257faiztiGiC2QtKLGpbnnEGta2doK
Sec-Agent-Operator: did:key:z6MkiY7Ng5cvV4gY5v3jL3k4m2n1p9q8r7s6t5u4v3w2x1y0z
Sec-Agent-Mandate: https://agents.agentgrid.dev/mandates/mnd_01HZX9R3K4P
Sec-Agent-Chain: https://agents.agentgrid.dev/mandates/mnd_01HZX9R3K4P/chain.json
Sec-Agent-Proof-Url: https://target-site.com/.well-known/agentgrid-verified
Signature-Input: sig1=("@authority" "@path" "@method" "content-digest" "sec-agent-identity" "sec-agent-mandate");created=1749741600;expires=1749741900;nonce="8f3e2a1b7c9d4e5f6a7b8c9d0e1f2a3b";keyid="did:key:z6MkhaXgBZDvotDkL5257faiztiGiC2QtKLGpbnnEGta2doK#key-1";alg="ed25519"
Signature: sig1=:dGhpcyBpcyBhIHNpZ25hdHVyZQ...==
```

#### Field-by-field semantics

| Header | Verifier action |
|---|---|
| `Sec-Agent-Identity` | The agent's DID — load public key from `.well-known` |
| `Sec-Agent-Operator` | The root operator DID — must match the chain's root issuer |
| `Sec-Agent-Mandate` | URL of the Mandate VC — fetch and cache; verify issuer's signature |
| `Sec-Agent-Chain` | URL of the full chain document (root → leaf); single fetch for verification |
| `Sec-Agent-Proof-Url` | Optional: URL where the site can POST a callback to register a verified interaction (e.g., for reputation build-up) |
| `Signature-Input` | RFC 9421 component list with freshness bounds; the `expires` and `nonce` are anti-replay |
| `Signature` | Ed25519 signature over the canonicalized `Signature-Input` components |

The `nonce` is 128 bits of randomness, included in the signed component set, and stored in a server-side dedupe cache keyed by `(keyid, nonce)` with a TTL matching `expires - created + 30s` slack.

### 4. The `.well-known` Directory — Key Publication

```
https://agents.agentgrid.dev/.well-known/http-message-signatures-directory/
  did:key:z6MkhaXgBZDvotDkL5257faiztiGiC2QtKLGpbnnEGta2doK
```

The URL is a literal — the DID after the prefix. (URL-encode the `:` as `%3A` per RFC 3986 if your server enforces it.) The response body:

```jsonc
{
  "@context": "https://agentgrid.dev/ns/agent-directory/v1",
  "id": "did:key:z6MkhaXgBZDvotDkL5257faiztiGiC2QtKLGpbnnEGta2doK",
  "operator": "did:key:z6MkiY7Ng5cvV4gY5v3jL3k4m2n1p9q8r7s6t5u4v3w2x1y0z",
  "displayName": "AgentGrid Agent — Research",
  "status": "active",
  "statusUpdatedAt": "2026-06-12T10:00:00Z",
  "verificationMethod": [{
    "id": "did:key:z6MkhaXgBZDvotDkL5257faiztiGiC2QtKLGpbnnEGta2doK#key-1",
    "type": "Ed25519VerificationKey2020",
    "publicKeyJwk": { "kty": "OKP", "crv": "Ed25519", "x": "..." },
    "validFrom": "2026-06-12T00:00:00Z"
  }, {
    "id": "did:key:z6MkhaXgBZDvotDkL5257faiztiGiC2QtKLGpbnnEGta2doK#key-2",
    "type": "Ed25519VerificationKey2020",
    "publicKeyJwk": { "kty": "OKP", "crv": "Ed25519", "x": "..." },
    "validFrom": "2026-09-01T00:00:00Z",
    "parentKeyId": "did:key:z6MkhaXgBZDvotDkL5257faiztiGiC2QtKLGpbnnEGta2doK#key-1",
    "crossProof": "z5Yp9N..." // key-1 signs key-2 to prove continuity
  }],
  "chainEndpoint": "https://agents.agentgrid.dev/.well-known/mandate-chains/did:key:z6MkhaXgBZDvotDkL5257faiztiGiC2QtKLGpbnnEGta2doK/root.json",
  "statusList": "https://agents.agentgrid.dev/credentials/status/did:key:z6Mk...",
  "proof": { /* operator-signed to bind the directory entry to the operator's key */ }
}
```

`Cache-Control: max-age=300, stale-while-revalidate=60` — short TTL so revocation propagates within five minutes without coupling the verifier to live calls.

### 5. The Attenuation Chain Endpoint

```
GET /v1/chains/{agentDid}/current
```

Response:

```jsonc
{
  "agentDid": "did:key:z6MkhaXgBZDvotDkL5257faiztiGiC2QtKLGpbnnEGta2doK",
  "rootOperator": "did:key:z6MkiY7Ng5cvV4gY5v3jL3k4m2n1p9q8r7s6t5u4v3w2x1y0z",
  "depth": 2,
  "nodes": [
    {
      "id": "mnd_01HZX8ROOT",
      "issuer": "did:key:z6MkiY7N...",
      "subject": "did:key:z6MkhaXgBZDvot...",
      "capability": "browse",
      "scope": { "allowedDomains": ["*"], "trustDomain": "agent-owned" },
      "delegable": true, "maxDelegationDepth": 3,
      "validFrom": "2026-01-01T00:00:00Z",
      "validUntil": "2027-01-01T00:00:00Z",
      "proof": "z3Jp7G4n..."
    },
    {
      "id": "mnd_01HZX9R3K4P",
      "parent": "mnd_01HZX8ROOT",
      "issuer": "did:key:z6MkhaXgBZDvot...",  // agent, not operator
      "subject": "did:key:z6Mkchild...",         // sub-agent
      "capability": "browse",
      "scope": { "allowedDomains": ["*.github.com"], "trustDomain": "agent-owned" },
      "delegable": false, "maxDelegationDepth": 0,
      "validFrom": "2026-06-12T00:00:00Z",
      "validUntil": "2026-12-31T23:59:59Z",
      "proof": "z4Kq8M2p..."
    }
  ],
  "subsetProof": {
    "type": "ScopeSubsetAttestation2026",
    "verifiedAt": "2026-06-13T12:00:00Z",
    "rules": [
      { "rule": "domain-subset", "child": ["*.github.com"], "parent": ["*"], "result": "pass" },
      { "rule": "time-tightening", "child.notBefore": "2026-06-12", "parent.notBefore": "2026-01-01", "result": "pass" },
      { "rule": "time-tightening", "child.expires": "2026-12-31", "parent.expires": "2027-01-01", "result": "pass" },
      { "rule": "depth-bounded", "child.depth": 1, "parent.maxDelegationDepth": 3, "result": "pass" }
    ]
  },
  "attestorSignature": "z7Mq3R9x..."  // AgentGrid registry signs the verified subset relations
}
```

### 6. The Attenuation Verifier (Mathematical Core)

The verifier is a pure function — `(child, parent) → {valid: boolean, violations: Violation[]}`. Properties are **property-tested** (fast-check) with thousands of randomized cases; a regression is a P0 incident.

```typescript
type Scope = {
  allowedDomains?: string[];   // "*" is the universal set
  deniedDomains?: string[];
  allowedCategories?: string[];
  deniedMerchants?: string[];
  limitPerTransactionMinor?: number;
  limitPerPeriodMinor?: number;
  periodStart?: string;        // ISO-8601
  notBefore?: string;
  expires?: string;
  trustDomain?: "agent-owned" | "user-owned";
  maxSessionMinutes?: number;
  // ... capability-specific
};

type Violation = { rule: string; message: string };

function isSubset(child: Scope, parent: Scope): Violation[] {
  const v: Violation[] = [];
  
  // 1. Domain allowlist monotonicity
  if (parent.allowedDomains && !parent.allowedDomains.includes("*")) {
    if (!child.allowedDomains) {
      v.push({ rule: "domain-subset", message: "child has no allowlist, parent does" });
    } else {
      for (const d of child.allowedDomains) {
        if (!parent.allowedDomains.some(p => domainMatches(p, d))) {
          v.push({ rule: "domain-subset", message: `child domain ${d} not in parent` });
        }
      }
    }
  }
  
  // 2. Denylist monotonicity (child denials must be a superset of parent denials)
  if (parent.deniedDomains) {
    for (const d of parent.deniedDomains) {
      if (!child.deniedDomains?.some(x => domainMatches(x, d))) {
        v.push({ rule: "deny-superset", message: `parent-denied ${d} must remain denied` });
      }
    }
  }
  
  // 3. Spend limits monotonic (child limits must be ≤ parent)
  if (parent.limitPerTransactionMinor != null && 
      child.limitPerTransactionMinor != null &&
      child.limitPerTransactionMinor > parent.limitPerTransactionMinor) {
    v.push({ rule: "tx-limit-monotonic", 
             message: `child tx limit ${child.limitPerTransactionMinor} > parent ${parent.limitPerTransactionMinor}` });
  }
  if (parent.limitPerPeriodMinor != null && 
      child.limitPerPeriodMinor != null &&
      child.limitPerPeriodMinor > parent.limitPerPeriodMinor) {
    v.push({ rule: "period-limit-monotonic", 
             message: `child period limit ${child.limitPerPeriodMinor} > parent ${parent.limitPerPeriodMinor}` });
  }
  
  // 4. Time bounds monotonic (child notBefore ≥ parent notBefore, child expires ≤ parent expires)
  if (parent.notBefore && child.notBefore && child.notBefore < parent.notBefore) {
    v.push({ rule: "time-tightening", 
             message: `child notBefore ${child.notBefore} precedes parent ${parent.notBefore}` });
  }
  if (parent.expires && child.expires && child.expires > parent.expires) {
    v.push({ rule: "time-tightening", 
             message: `child expires ${child.expires} extends past parent ${parent.expires}` });
  }
  
  // 5. Trust domain: child cannot escalate from agent-owned to user-owned
  if (parent.trustDomain === "agent-owned" && child.trustDomain === "user-owned") {
    v.push({ rule: "trust-escalation", 
             message: "child cannot escalate to user-owned trust domain" });
  }
  
  // 6. Session minutes monotonic
  if (parent.maxSessionMinutes != null && child.maxSessionMinutes != null &&
      child.maxSessionMinutes > parent.maxSessionMinutes) {
    v.push({ rule: "session-monotonic", 
             message: `child session minutes ${child.maxSessionMinutes} > parent ${parent.maxSessionMinutes}` });
  }
  
  // 7. Categories monotonic (child allowed ⊆ parent allowed, child denied ⊇ parent denied)
  // ... same pattern
  
  return v;
}
```

**Domain matching note:** `domainMatches(pattern, host)` implements a glob where `*.example.com` matches any subdomain of `example.com` and `example.com` itself; `*` matches anything. A wildcards-only allowlist (no literal domains) is normalized at issuance time.

**Chain walk:**

```typescript
async function verifyChain(chain: MandateNode[]): Promise<{valid: boolean, errors: string[]}> {
  const errors: string[] = [];
  
  if (chain.length === 0) {
    return { valid: false, errors: ["empty chain"] };
  }
  
  // 1. Root must be operator-signed (issuer DID == passport.operatorDid)
  const root = chain[0];
  const passport = await fetchPassport(root.subject);
  if (root.issuer !== passport.operator) {
    errors.push(`root issuer ${root.issuer} != passport operator ${passport.operator}`);
  }
  
  // 2. Each node's Ed25519 signature must verify against its declared issuer
  for (let i = 0; i < chain.length; i++) {
    const node = chain[i];
    const issuerPubKey = await resolveVerificationKey(node.issuer, node.proof.verificationMethod);
    if (!verifyJcsSignature(issuerPubKey, node, node.proof.proofValue)) {
      errors.push(`node[${i}] signature invalid`);
    }
  }
  
  // 3. Monotonic attenuation: each child must be a subset of its parent
  for (let i = 1; i < chain.length; i++) {
    const violations = isSubset(chain[i].scope, chain[i-1].scope);
    if (violations.length > 0) {
      errors.push(`node[${i}] violates parent node[${i-1}]: ${JSON.stringify(violations)}`);
    }
    if (chain[i].validFrom < chain[i-1].validFrom) {
      errors.push(`node[${i}] validFrom precedes parent`);
    }
    if (chain[i].validUntil > chain[i-1].validUntil) {
      errors.push(`node[${i}] validUntil extends past parent`);
    }
  }
  
  // 4. Depth-bounded
  if (chain.length - 1 > chain[0].maxDelegationDepth) {
    errors.push(`chain depth ${chain.length-1} exceeds root maxDelegationDepth ${chain[0].maxDelegationDepth}`);
  }
  
  // 5. No cycles (parent pointer must reference earlier node, not later)
  for (let i = 1; i < chain.length; i++) {
    if (!chain[i].parent) {
      errors.push(`node[${i}] has no parent reference`);
    } else {
      const parentIdx = chain.findIndex(n => n.id === chain[i].parent);
      if (parentIdx < 0 || parentIdx >= i) {
        errors.push(`node[${i}] parent ${chain[i].parent} not an earlier node in chain`);
      }
    }
  }
  
  // 6. Capability consistency — chain cannot change capability mid-flight
  for (let i = 1; i < chain.length; i++) {
    if (chain[i].capability !== chain[i-1].capability) {
      errors.push(`node[${i}] capability ${chain[i].capability} != parent ${chain[i-1].capability}`);
    }
  }
  
  return { valid: errors.length === 0, errors };
}
```

**Property tests (fast-check):**

```typescript
fc.assert(fc.property(
  fc.scopeArbitrary(), fc.scopeArbitrary(),
  (a, b) => {
    // If b isSubset(a), then a.isSubset(b) must be false UNLESS a == b
    const aSubB = isSubset(b, a);
    if (aSubB.length === 0 && !scopeEqual(a, b)) {
      // b is a strict subset of a → a cannot be a subset of b
      const bSubA = isSubset(a, b);
      return bSubA.length > 0;
    }
    return true;
  }
));

fc.assert(fc.property(
  fc.chainArbitrary({maxDepth: 5}),
  (chain) => {
    // Verifying a chain is idempotent
    const r1 = verifyChain(chain);
    const r2 = verifyChain(chain);
    return JSON.stringify(r1) === JSON.stringify(r2);
  }
));
```

### 7. The Site Verification Endpoint (Hosted Fallback)

Sites that want live verification (preferred for high-value flows) can call:

```
POST /v1/verify
Content-Type: application/json

{
  "agentDid": "did:key:z6Mk...",
  "operatorDid": "did:key:z6MkiY7N...",
  "mandateId": "mnd_01HZX9R3K4P",
  "action": {
    "capability": "pay",
    "amountMinor": 5000,
    "currency": "USD",
    "merchant": "github.com",
    "category": "saas"
  },
  "nonce": "8f3e2a1b7c9d4e5f6a7b8c9d0e1f2a3b",
  "issuedAt": 1749741600,
  "expiresAt": 1749741900,
  "requestSignature": "base64..."
}
```

Response:

```jsonc
{
  "valid": true,
  "agentDid": "did:key:z6Mk...",
  "operatorDid": "did:key:z6MkiY7N...",
  "mandateId": "mnd_01HZX9R3K4P",
  "chainVerified": true,
  "chainDepth": 2,
  "attenuation": {
    "monotonic": true,
    "violations": []
  },
  "scopeCheck": {
    "merchantAllowed": true,
    "categoryAllowed": true,
    "underLimit": true,
    "underPeriodCap": true,
    "stepUpRequired": false
  },
  "statusCheck": {
    "agentStatus": "active",
    "mandateStatus": "active",
    "lastUpdated": "2026-06-13T12:00:00Z"
  },
  "policyDecision": "ALLOW",
  "decisionId": "dec_01HZXA7Y2K",
  "issuedAt": 1749741601,
  "expiresAt": 1749741901,
  "attestorSignature": "z5Rt8W3k..."
}
```

The `attestorSignature` is the AgentGrid registry's Ed25519 signature over `JCS(response without signature)`; sites can pin the registry's public key and verify offline, eliminating per-request callback latency in the hot path.

### 8. StatusList2021 (Embedded Revocation)

The agent's passport and each mandate embed a `credentialStatus` block pointing to a bitstring status list. The verifier downloads the list once, caches it (`max-age=120`), and checks one bit per credential. Revocation is O(1) per check, and the status list itself is signed by the AgentGrid registry.

```jsonc
{
  "@context": ["https://www.w3.org/ns/credentials/v2"],
  "id": "https://agents.agentgrid.dev/credentials/status/did:key:z6Mk...#0",
  "type": ["VerifiableCredential", "StatusList2021Credential"],
  "credentialSubject": {
    "id": "https://agents.agentgrid.dev/credentials/status/did:key:z6Mk...#list",
    "type": "StatusList2021",
    "statusPurpose": "revocation",
    "encodedList": "H4sIAAAAAAAAA-3BMQEAAADCoPVPbQwfoAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAIC3gZMcAQAAAA"
  },
  "proof": { /* registry signature */ }
}
```

### 9. DNS Schema (Optional Discovery Shortcut)

For sites that want to verify without parsing the chain URL out of headers:

```
TXT _agentgrid.agentgrid.dev "v=agd1; registry=https://agents.agentgrid.dev; attestor-pubkey=ed25519:z6MkiY7N..."
```

A site that sees a request with `Sec-Agent-Identity: did:key:...` can do a single DNS lookup to confirm the registry domain and pin the attestor key, defending against a malicious actor pointing a `.well-known` directory at an attacker-controlled host.

### 10. Concrete Site-Side Verification Pseudocode

```typescript
import { verify as verifyEd25519 } from "@noble/ed25519";
import { canonicalize } from "canonicalize";

async function verifyAgentRequest(req: Request): Promise<VerificationResult> {
  const agentDid = req.headers["sec-agent-identity"];
  const operatorDid = req.headers["sec-agent-operator"];
  const mandateUrl = req.headers["sec-agent-mandate"];
  const chainUrl = req.headers["sec-agent-chain"];
  const sigInput = req.headers["signature-input"];
  const signature = req.headers["signature"];

  // 1. Parse Signature-Input (RFC 9421)
  const { components, created, expires, nonce, keyid, alg } = parseSigInput(sigInput);
  if (alg !== "ed25519") return { valid: false, reason: "alg-unsupported" };
  if (Date.now()/1000 > expires) return { valid: false, reason: "expired" };
  if (Date.now()/1000 < created - 300) return { valid: false, reason: "not-yet-valid" };
  if (await nonceSeen(nonce)) return { valid: false, reason: "nonce-replay" };

  // 2. Fetch agent directory
  const dir = await fetchAgentDirectory(agentDid);
  if (dir.status !== "active") return { valid: false, reason: "agent-not-active" };
  if (dir.operator !== operatorDid) return { valid: false, reason: "operator-mismatch" };

  // 3. Fetch and verify the Mandate chain
  const chain = await fetchChain(chainUrl);
  const chainResult = await verifyChain(chain.nodes);
  if (!chainResult.valid) return { valid: false, reason: "chain-invalid", details: chainResult.errors };

  // 4. Verify RFC 9421 signature over the canonicalized components
  const pubKey = dir.verificationMethod.find(k => k.id === keyid);
  if (!pubKey) return { valid: false, reason: "unknown-key" };
  const sigBase = buildSignatureBase(req, components, created, expires, nonce, keyid);
  const sigBytes = base64Decode(signature.replace(/^.*:/, "").replace(/:$/, ""));
  const valid = await verifyEd25519(sigBytes, new TextEncoder().encode(sigBase), 
                                     jwkToBytes(pubKey.publicKeyJwk));
  if (!valid) return { valid: false, reason: "signature-invalid" };

  // 5. Cross-check mandate issuer == agent key
  const leafMandate = chain.nodes[chain.nodes.length - 1];
  if (leafMandate.issuer !== agentDid) return { valid: false, reason: "mandate-issuer-mismatch" };

  // 6. Check StatusList2021 revocation (or trust Cache-Control TTL)
  if (await isMandateRevoked(leafMandate.id)) return { valid: false, reason: "mandate-revoked" };

  // 7. Record nonce to prevent replay
  await recordNonce(agentDid, nonce, expires);

  return { 
    valid: true, 
    agentDid, operatorDid,
    chainDepth: chain.nodes.length,
    leafMandate: leafMandate.id,
    capability: leafMandate.capability,
    scope: leafMandate.scope
  };
}
```

### 11. WAF / Bot-Defense Integration Patterns

| WAF / Bot Defense | Integration |
|---|---|
| **Cloudflare** | Native: agents signed with the AgentGrid keypair (via the executor's HTTP client) are automatically detected by Cloudflare's Web Bot Auth; CF applies the configured "Verified Agents" policy. Sites enable in dashboard → Security → Bots → "Allow verified agents" → add AgentGrid as a registered agent identity provider. |
| **AWS WAF Bot Control** | Custom rule matching `Signature` header presence + JCS verification via Lambda@Edge; verified requests tagged `awswaf:associated-rule-list:agentgrid-verified` and exempted from CAPTCHA. |
| **Akamai Bot Manager** | Custom request enrichment header; use Akamai's pre-action hook to call `/v1/verify` and tag the session. |
| **Fastly / Varnish** | Edge Compute function that runs the verification pseudocode; sets a `req.http.X-Agent-Grid-Verified = "1"` flag for downstream origin logic. |
| **Haproxy / Nginx** | `auth_request` directive to a sidecar verifier microservice. |
| **Custom sites (Node/Go/Python)** | Use the published `agentgrid-verifier-sdk-{lang}` package — single function call wraps the entire flow. |

### 12. The Mandate ↔ Request Coupling

The `Signature-Input` includes `sec-agent-mandate` as a **covered component** — the URL is part of the signed bytes. This binds the request to a specific mandate ID, so an attacker cannot replay a request with a different (more permissive) mandate by swapping the `Sec-Agent-Mandate` header. The site also fetches the mandate, verifies its `credentialSubject.id` matches the `sec-agent-identity`, and that the mandate's `credentialSubject.id` (the agent DID) matches the chain's leaf node subject.

---

## ⚠️ FAILURE MODES & TRUST HARDENING

### 1. Identity Spoofing

**Attack:** Attacker forges a `Sec-Agent-Identity` header pointing to a DID they do not control.

**Defense in depth:**
- The `.well-known` directory entry is **operator-signed**, binding the public key to the operator DID. A forger cannot publish a directory entry without the operator's key.
- The `did:key` method is self-certifying: the public key is derived from the identifier's multibase + multicodec suffix. An attacker who picks a random DID string cannot produce a valid Ed25519 signature verifiable under it.
- Sites must verify the signature over the canonicalized `Signature-Input` using the **fetched** public key from the directory, never a key supplied in the request.

**Residual risk:** A site that fails to fetch the directory and trusts a header-supplied key is compromised by design. SDKs must **default-deny** if the fetch fails (with a configurable allowlist for offline-only verification modes).

### 2. The 16.2.2-Style Forgery

**Attack:** An attacker tampers with a stored passport file to swap the `verificationMethod` pointer to a key they control, then re-issues a chain that validates cryptographically but roots at the attacker's operator.

**Defense:** The `verifyChain` algorithm cross-references the passport's `operator` field against the root mandate's `issuer` field, both fetched from independent sources (`.well-known` and the chain endpoint). The directory entry is operator-signed; the chain's root mandate is operator-signed; the two must agree. The verifier rejects any chain whose root issuer does not match the directory-declared operator.

**Property test:** For any passport `P` and any operator DID `O' != P.operator`, the chain rooted at `O'` for `P.subject` is rejected.

### 3. Key Compromise

**Attack:** The agent's Ed25519 private key leaks (memory dump, vault breach, prompt-injection-induced exfiltration).

**Defense:**
- **Key rotation with cross-proof:** New key carries `parentKeyId` and `crossProof` — the old key signs the new public key, proving continuity. Sites verify the chain `old → new` and accept the new key.
- **Overlap window:** Both keys valid for a configurable grace period (default 1 hour) to allow cache invalidation; old key can be explicitly revoked sooner via the status list.
- **Vault isolation:** The signing key is sign-only (`sign()` returns bytes, never the key); production deployments use HSM/KMS where the key bytes physically cannot be extracted. Even a fully compromised agent process cannot read the key.
- **Blast-radius cap:** Short token lifetimes and short `Signature-Input` validity windows (default 5 minutes) mean a stolen key has a brief exploitation window before rotation propagates.

### 4. Replay Attacks

**Attack:** Attacker captures a valid signed request and replays it later.

**Defense (all required):**
- **Nonce:** 128-bit random, included in the signed `Signature-Input`, server-side dedupe cache with TTL = `expires - created + 30s` slack.
- **Timestamp:** `created` and `expires` in the signature input; reject `expires < now` and `created > now + 300s` (clock skew leeway).
- **Component coverage:** `sec-agent-mandate`, `sec-agent-identity`, `@path`, `@method`, `@authority`, `content-digest` are all covered; swapping any field invalidates the signature.
- **Mandate status:** A replayed request after mandate revocation is caught by StatusList2021; the 5-minute cache TTL bounds the gap.

**Property test:** Any byte mutation in a covered component causes `verifyEd25519` to return `false`. Any nonce reuse within TTL returns `{valid: false, reason: "nonce-replay"}`.

### 5. Operator Binding Forgery (Refined)

**Attack:** A compromised agent generates a *new* passport with itself as the operator, signs a root mandate, and presents it to a site.

**Defense:** The site fetched the directory entry from `agents.agentgrid.dev/.well-known/.../did:key:AGENT`. The directory is **operator-signed** — only a request to a directory entry that an *actual registered human* has authorized can declare that agent. The agent cannot self-register a directory entry; the operator (via the trusted on-device app) does. A forged passport signed by an attacker-controlled operator will:
- Be served at a *different* registry domain (attacker cannot write to `agents.agentgrid.dev`).
- Fail the operator cross-check (`directory.operator != request.sec-agent-operator`).
- Be rejectable via a CSP-like allowlist of known registry hostnames.

**Defense at the site:** Pin `agents.agentgrid.dev` as the registry hostname; reject requests referencing other registries unless explicitly allowlisted.

### 6. Mandate Chain Forgery (Attenuation Bypass)

**Attack:** A compromised agent mints a child mandate whose scope is *wider* than the parent's, hoping the site will only check the leaf.

**Defense:** The site's verifier **must walk the entire chain** (`chain.nodes`) and apply `verifyChain()` before trusting the leaf scope. The AgentGrid attestor signature on the chain document is itself a third-party guarantee that the subset relations hold — a site that pins the AgentGrid attestor public key can skip walking the chain *only* if it trusts AgentGrid to have walked it correctly. Defensive sites walk it themselves; the property tests are open-source for inspection.

**Property test:** For any two scopes `A`, `B`, if `isSubset(B, A)` returns `[]` and `!scopeEqual(A, B)`, then `verifyChain([A, B])` will succeed in chain building but fail in the scope check.

### 7. Hard Spend-Cap Bypass (16.2.3-class)

**Attack:** Agent requests spend above `limitPerTransactionMinor`; legacy systems marked it STEP_UP, and a hijacked approval channel (or absent operator) could rubber-stamp it.

**Defense:** The policy engine treats mandate ceilings as **strict hard limits** — `amountMinor > limitPerTransactionMinor` is a DENY at the policy layer, not a STEP_UP. This rule fires *before* the STEP_UP path. The site's `scopeCheck.underLimit` must also enforce the limit regardless of the `policyDecision` field — defense in depth.

### 8. Null/Type-Confusion Bypass (16.2.4-class)

**Attack:** Agent sends `amountMinor: null` or `undefined`; `null > limit` evaluates to `false`, the check passes.

**Defense:** The verifier's schema validation rejects any request where `amountMinor` is not a non-negative integer. Use a Zod/Valibot schema at the policy engine boundary; reject and log. Sites should also reject malformed `sec-agent-*` headers (missing required fields, unparseable timestamps) before fetching the chain.

### 9. Revocation Propagation

**Attack:** Operator revokes the agent, but a cached directory entry lets the agent continue to make requests for hours.

**Defense:**
- **StatusList2021** embedded in credentials: revocation bit flips within seconds, O(1) check.
- **Directory TTL:** `max-age=300` (5 minutes) + `stale-while-revalidate=60` — sites see revocation within ~5 minutes worst case.
- **CDN purging on revocation:** Pushing a purge to the CDN edge on `status=revoked` write; signed purge request to Fastly/Cloudflare.
- **Long-poll / SSE fallback for high-value sites:** Sites can subscribe to `GET /v1/revocations/stream?agent={did}` to receive push notifications.

**Failure mode:** If AgentGrid is unreachable during a request, sites fall back to the cached directory entry (with the 5-minute TTL). The signer (Ed25519) and the chain are still cryptographically valid; only the revocation check degrades. For *high-risk* actions (financial), sites should **fail closed** if they cannot reach the status list and the cached entry is past TTL. For *low-risk* actions (read), **fail open** is acceptable. Sites configure the policy via `Sec-Agent-Risk-Profile` annotation in their WAF.

### 10. CAPTCHA / Turnstile Fallback

**Attack / scenario:** A site uses Cloudflare Turnstile, and even with a valid signed agent, the site chooses to challenge.

**Defense:**
- **Polite signal:** Sites receiving a verified signed request can inspect `Sec-Agent-Identity` and skip the Turnstile challenge. The site publishes a policy: "We honor signed agents from these registrars: [AgentGrid, ...]."
- **Agent-side fallback:** If the site returns a Turnstile challenge page (HTTP 200 with Turnstile widget, or 403 with `cf-mitigated: challenge`), the executor's browser primitive detects this and triggers **STEP_UP to the human** with a pre-formatted prompt: "Site X is showing a CAPTCHA. Tap to solve." The human solves it once; the browser session is propagated; the agent continues. This is the *one tap on a hard wall* pattern.
- **Site-side opt-in:** Sites can request human verification on high-stakes actions regardless of agent verification, by setting a policy header. The agent receives a 401 with `Sec-Agent-Required-Step-Up: human-only` and routes to the approval channel.

**Refusal of detection-evasion:** The executor must never attempt to bypass Turnstile via automation, headless-browser fingerprint manipulation, or CAPTCHA-solving services. Doing so:
- Violates the site's ToS.
- Erodes the very signed-agent ecosystem that gives this approach its leverage.
- Triggers IP-level blocks that affect future legitimate use.

### 11. Rate Limiting & Anomaly Detection

**Per-agent:** 60 requests/minute, 1000/hour, with burst to 120/min. Configurable per Mandate.

**Per-site:** Sites enforce their own limits. AgentGrid publishes a `Sec-Agent-Usage-Hint` header (signed) carrying the agent's typical request profile so sites can set thresholds that admit good behavior.

**Anomaly detection (CAEP-style):** Sudden change in target domains, request frequency, or geographic egress triggers a STEP_UP request to the operator ("Unusual activity from your agent — confirm to continue"). The executor blocks pending the response; if denied, the agent is auto-suspended pending operator review.

### 12. Registry Compromise

**Attack:** `agents.agentgrid.dev` is compromised; attacker serves forged directory entries.

**Defense:**
- **DNSSEC + CAA records** pinning TLS issuance to a specific CA.
- **Public key pinning** in the SDK: sites ship with a pinned `agents.agentgrid.dev` leaf certificate or public key.
- **M-of-N attestor quorum** for the StatusList2021 publication: the status list is co-signed by ≥3 of 5 geographically-distributed attestor keys; a single compromise cannot forge a "valid" status entry.
- **Out-of-band hash publication:** Daily Merkle root of all status list updates published to a transparency log (e.g., Sigstore Rekor); operators can monitor for unauthorized state changes.

### 13. Spec Drift & Replay Across Key Generations

**Attack:** Old signed request is replayed after the agent rotates keys.

**Defense:** The verifier checks the signature against the *currently valid* key for the `keyid`. If the old key has been retired (not in the directory's `verificationMethod` array), the signature is rejected. The overlap window is short (1 hour default), and rotation events are themselves signed and published to the directory with a new `statusUpdatedAt`.

### 14. CAEP / Continuous Evaluation

When a site's trust in the agent changes (e.g., anomalous activity report from another site), it can publish a CAEP-style event. AgentGrid's stream endpoint (`/v1/events/stream?agent={did}`) carries:
- `agent-suspension` (status → suspended; all signing paused)
- `agent-revocation` (status → revoked; chain purged from cache)
- `mandate-amendment` (new mandate supersedes old; sites should re-fetch)

Sites subscribed to the stream react within seconds; sites polling do so within their configured interval.

### 15. The Honest Residual

**Semantic intent attacks remain undefeated.** A hijacked agent that operates *within* a valid, non-revoked, attenuated, properly-signed chain can still do in-policy-but-harmful things. The transmission layer proves *who* and *what is authorized*; it does not prove *whether the reasoning was hijacked*. The Mandate is the **envelope**, not the **intuition** — the human-operator-as-judgment layer (STEP_UP on irreversible or high-salience actions) is the only available mitigation. Sites can mark `Sec-Agent-Required-Step-Up: high-salience` on the response to force this for their own risk model, and AgentGrid will route through the approval channel. The standard itself (RFC 9421 + W3C VC + StatusList2021) is sufficient to make the agent **accountable, scoped, and revocable**; making it **wise** is a separate research problem and is not overpromised.

---
